issue.title: B: Frozen display across surfaces (detail badge, pipeline, watcher debounce)

successCriteria.description: Detail GitHub badge re-fetches on stateChanged (refresh nonce) so a close-elsewhere updates without reselection
Rust watcher uses trailing-edge debounce (<=100ms) so the final write of a burst is always emitted (no freeze on intermediate state)
work.phase cleared on completion in BOTH state.mjs AND state_transition.rs, guarded by parity tests
pnpm build + cargo clippy --deny warnings + all test suites green

phases.title: Detail re-fetch on stateChanged (refresh nonce)
phases.content: App.tsx: add a detailRefreshNonce that increments on every stateChanged file event; thread it into the on-demand fetch_github_issue_by_number effect deps so the selected issue's GitHub state (and thus the deriveDisplayStatus badge) refreshes without reselection. Pairs with #222 (selector) and #218→A (release children now in recentIssues).
verification: App.tsx fetch effect deps include detailRefreshNonce; nonce incremented in the tiki-file-changed stateChanged handler
phases.summary: App.tsx: detailRefreshNonce increments on stateChanged and feeds the on-demand issue fetch deps — badge updates without reselection.

phases.title: Rust watcher: leading-edge → trailing-edge debounce
phases.content: watcher.rs: replace the leading-edge should_emit with a trailing-edge scheme — pending HashMap<key,(event,Instant)>, poll at 50ms, flush a target only after it goes quiet for DEBOUNCE (100ms). Guarantees the final write of a burst (e.g. release teardown's remove+append-history) reaches the frontend instead of freezing on an intermediate write. Pure quiet_keys() helper + unit tests.
verification: watcher.rs no longer has leading-edge should_emit; flush_quiet_events + quiet_keys present
phases.summary: watcher.rs: leading-edge should_emit replaced with trailing-edge pending-map + quiet_keys/flush_quiet_events (100ms); 2 unit tests.

phases.title: Clear stale work.phase on completion (Node + Rust parity) + verification
phases.content: state.mjs and state_transition.rs: clear phase when toStatus === completed (next to the existing parallelExecution clear), so the timeline/sidebar don't show a stale '1/N'. Parity tests: Rust test_phase_cleared_on_completed + Node 'phase is cleared ... (#220)' spawn test. Run full build + clippy + cargo test + node --test + desktop vitest.
verification: state.mjs deletes entry.phase on completed; state_transition.rs sets ctx.phase = None on Completed
Rust test_phase_cleared_on_completed + Node phase-clear test green; clippy --deny warnings clean; pnpm build clean; desktop vitest green
phases.summary: phase cleared on completed in state.mjs + state_transition.rs; Rust + Node parity tests. build/clippy/cargo/node/vitest all green.
